title: Oxalis obliqua
taxonomy: Oxalis obliqua is classified in the kingdom Plantae, specifically within the family Oxalidaceae. This genus Oxalis is further divided into various species, some of which are native to South America. Oxalis obliqua belongs to the order Oxalidales and is commonly referred to as a wood sorrel. It is a type of flowering plant, characterized by its small white or pink flowers and heart-shaped leaves. The classification of Oxalis obliqua reflects its botanical relationships and evolutionary history.

properties: Oxalis obliqua, a species of wood sorrel, is known for its potential therapeutic properties. preparations: Oxalis obliqua is a species of wood sorrel, and while specific herbal preparation information is limited, some species within the Oxalis genus have been used in traditional medicine for their astringent and anti-inflammatory properties. intro: Oxalis obliqua, also known as the purple wood sorrel or purple oxalis, is a perennial plant species native to the tropical forests of Central and South America. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include potential health benefits such as anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, as well as its antimicrobial and antiseptic properties. The bioactive constituents of Oxalis obliqua include anthocyanins, flavonoids, and tannins, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, the plant is often prepared as a decoction, infusion, or tincture, and is used to treat various conditions, including wounds, skin infections, and respiratory issues.
